title: Cross-Facility Referral Loses Patient Identity Continuity
scenario: A patient referral is received from another facility, but the receiving system cannot bind the referral to a unique local patient identity.
initial_state: The referral was transmitted with an external patient identifier.
state_transition: The receiving facility maps the referral to multiple possible local records.
execution_boundary: The referred clinical action is evaluated before execution.
constitutional_evaluation: Patient identity continuity must be preserved across facility boundary transitions.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because the external identity cannot be deterministically bound to one local patient record.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when cross-facility identity continuity cannot be reconstructed.
